execution_prompt.title: AO File Rename + GPU Rewrite + Config Resolver Fix

comprehension_gate.instruction: Before ANY implementation work, read this ENTIRE prompt, then present a structured summary to the Lead Architect: (a) Milestone objective in your own words. (b) List of work items with 1-sentence descriptions. (c) Files you will modify/create. (d) Test strategy. (e) Any risks or ambiguities you see. Then STOP and WAIT for the Lead Architect to approve before proceeding. Do NOT write code, create branches, or run commands until explicit approval is received.

execution_prompt.objective: Rename the Assistant Orchestrator (AO) inference module from NPU to GPU, rewrite load_model() for Qwen3-14B with speculative decoding (mirroring the PA pattern from M5.1), overhaul the entrypoint.py config resolver to read the [gpu] TOML section (fixing the GATEWAY_HANDSHAKE_FAILED root cause), update TOML configs to point to the Qwen3-14B model, and update AO constants.

references.ref: ADR-011: All LLM inference on GPU. NPU retired from P1 Core Loop.
ADR-012: Qwen3-14B target model. Speculative decoding mandatory. Draft model: Qwen3-0.6B INT4.
M5.1 completed: PA gpu_inference.py has the reference pattern for speculative decoding wiring.

attachments.file: services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py (M5.1 completed — reference pattern for load_model() speculative decoding wiring)

branch.name: feature/p5-task5-m5.2-ao-gpu-rewrite
branch.instruction: git checkout feature/p5-task5-m5.1-pa-spec-decode git checkout -b feature/p5-task5-m5.2-ao-gpu-rewrite

item.title: File rename: npu_inference.py → gpu_inference.py
item.description: Rename the AO inference module via git mv.
item.command: cd services/assistant_orchestrator/src git mv npu_inference.py gpu_inference.py
item.note: This is a tracked rename. git will preserve history. All subsequent edits in this milestone target the new filename gpu_inference.py.

item.title: Class rename: OrchestratorNPUInference → OrchestratorGPUInference
item.description: In services/assistant_orchestrator/src/gpu_inference.py (the renamed file): 1. Rename class OrchestratorNPUInference → OrchestratorGPUInference. 2. At the END of the file (after the class definition), add a backward- compatibility alias: # Backward-compatibility alias for external consumers (M5.3 cleans up). OrchestratorNPUInference = OrchestratorGPUInference 3. Update all NPU references in the class docstring, method docstrings, and log messages to say GPU.

item.title: Update __init__() to accept draft_model_dir
item.description:
In OrchestratorGPUInference.__init__():
CURRENT signature (line ~259): def __init__( self, model_dir: str, device: str = "NPU", priority: int = NPU_PRIORITY, max_tokens: int = OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP, manifest_path: str | None = None, ) -> None:
CHANGE TO: def __init__( self, model_dir: str, device: str = "GPU", priority: int = NPU_PRIORITY, max_tokens: int = OUTPUT_TOKEN_CAP, manifest_path: str | None = None, draft_model_dir: str | None = None, ) -> None:
Add in the body after self._manifest_path = manifest_path: self._draft_model_dir = ( Path(draft_model_dir) if draft_model_dir else Path(DRAFT_MODEL_OV_PATH) )
IMPORTS REQUIRED (add to the existing import block from shared.constants): from shared.constants import ( DRAFT_MODEL_OV_PATH, SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED, NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS, ) Note: Keep all existing imports from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.constants unchanged.

item.title: Rewrite load_model() for GPU + speculative decoding
item.description:
In OrchestratorGPUInference.load_model(), replace the LLMPipeline initialization block. Mirror the EXACT pattern from PA's services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py load_model() (M5.1).
CURRENT CODE (lines ~390-412 in npu_inference.py): try: priority_hint = "MEDIUM" if self._priority <= 1 else "LOW" config: dict[str, str] = { "PERFORMANCE_HINT": "LATENCY", "MODEL_PRIORITY": priority_hint, } self._pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(self._model_dir), self._device, **config, ) except TypeError: try: self._pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(self._model_dir), self._device, ) except Exception as e: ... except Exception as e: ...
REPLACE WITH (mirror PA pattern): try: priority_hint = "MEDIUM" if self._priority <= 1 else "LOW" config: dict[str, str] = { "PERFORMANCE_HINT": "LATENCY", "MODEL_PRIORITY": priority_hint, "INFERENCE_PRECISION_HINT": "f16", "GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": "ON", }
# Speculative decoding (ADR-012 §2.6): draft model + scheduler. pipeline_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {} if SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED: draft_model_xml = self._draft_model_dir / "openvino_model.xml" if draft_model_xml.exists(): pipeline_kwargs["draft_model"] = ov_genai.draft_model( str(self._draft_model_dir), self._device, ) scheduler_config = ov_genai.SchedulerConfig() scheduler_config.cache_size = 3 scheduler_config.enable_prefix_caching = False pipeline_kwargs["scheduler_config"] = scheduler_config logger.info( "Speculative decoding enabled: draft=%s, cache_size=3", self._draft_model_dir, ) else: logger.warning( "Draft model not found at %s — speculative decoding DISABLED. " "AO will run without draft acceleration.", self._draft_model_dir, )
self._pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(self._model_dir), self._device, **config, **pipeline_kwargs, ) except TypeError: # Some OpenVINO GenAI builds may not accept compile hints or spec-decode kwargs. logger.warning( "LLMPipeline rejected compile/spec-decode kwargs (older OV GenAI build) — " "speculative decoding NOT applied. Retrying without hints.", ) try: self._pipeline = ov_genai.LLMPipeline( str(self._model_dir), self._device, ) except Exception as e: # noqa: BLE001 logger.error( "Failed to initialize LLMPipeline for '%s': %s", self._device, e, ) return False except Exception as e: # noqa: BLE001 logger.error( "Failed to initialize LLMPipeline for '%s': %s", self._device, e, ) return False
RETAIN the existing weight integrity check, model file existence check, _load_tokenizer() call, and success log — only the LLMPipeline init block changes.
Update the error message in the "not available" check from "cannot load NPU model" → "cannot load GPU model" Update the success log from "Orchestrator NPU model loaded" → "Orchestrator GPU model loaded"

item.title: Update GenerationConfig defaults for deterministic generation
item.description:
In the GenerationConfig dataclass (lines ~214-233 in npu_inference.py):
CURRENT: temperature: float = 0.7 top_k: int = 50 top_p: float = 0.9 repetition_penalty: float = 1.1 do_sample: bool = True
CHANGE TO: temperature: float = 0.0 top_k: int = 0 top_p: float = 1.0 repetition_penalty: float = 1.0 do_sample: bool = False
Update the docstring to reflect Qwen3-14B INT4 deterministic generation: """Configuration for autoregressive generation.
Defaults for Qwen3-14B INT4 deterministic generation (ADR-012): - ``temperature=0.0``: greedy/deterministic. - ``top_k=0``: disabled (greedy mode). - ``top_p=1.0``: neutral (greedy mode). - ``repetition_penalty=1.0``: disabled (greedy mode). - ``do_sample=False``: greedy argmax. """

item.title: Wire num_assistant_tokens and stop_token_ids in _build_generation_config
item.description:
In _build_generation_config() (lines ~810-830 in npu_inference.py):
The method already sets stop_token_ids=[QWEN3_IM_END_TOKEN_ID] and stop_strings={"<|im_end|>"}.
ADD (after the stop_token_ids block, before the return): # Speculative decoding: num_assistant_tokens (DEC-01). if SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED: try: gen_config.num_assistant_tokens = NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS except Exception: pass
This ensures the draft model proposes NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS (3) tokens per verification step.

item.title: Update AO constants.py with MODEL_DIR
item.description:
In services/assistant_orchestrator/src/constants.py:
The file currently re-exports from shared.constants but does NOT define a MODEL_DIR constant. The model_dir is loaded from TOML config at runtime. No MODEL_DIR constant change is needed here.
HOWEVER, add the following imports from shared.constants to be available if needed by the renamed gpu_inference.py:
Add to the existing import block from shared.constants: DRAFT_MODEL_OV_PATH, SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED, NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS, TARGET_MODEL_OV_PATH,
And re-export them: DRAFT_MODEL_DIR: str = DRAFT_MODEL_OV_PATH SPECULATIVE_DECODING: bool = SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED ASSISTANT_TOKENS: int = NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS MODEL_DIR: str = TARGET_MODEL_OV_PATH
IMPORTANT: The gpu_inference.py module should import DRAFT_MODEL_OV_PATH, SPECULATIVE_DECODING_ENABLED, and NUM_ASSISTANT_TOKENS DIRECTLY from shared.constants (not via AO constants.py). This keeps the import path parallel with the PA pattern. The re-exports above are for external consumers only.

item.title: Overhaul entrypoint.py config resolver: npu → gpu
item.description:
This is the CRITICAL fix for the GATEWAY_HANDSHAKE_FAILED regression. The TOML configs already use [gpu] section headers (updated during ADR-011), but entrypoint.py still reads the "npu" section.
CHANGES in services/assistant_orchestrator/src/entrypoint.py:
A) UPDATE IMPORT (line ~38): CURRENT: from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.npu_inference import ( GenerationConfig, OrchestratorNPUInference, ) CHANGE TO: from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.gpu_inference import ( GenerationConfig, OrchestratorGPUInference, )
B) UPDATE _load_entrypoint_config() (lines ~337-344): CURRENT: npu = config_data.get("npu", {}) ... model_dir = self._resolve_path(repo_root, service_root, str(npu.get("model_dir", ""))) manifest_raw = str(npu.get("weight_manifest", "")).strip() ... device=str(npu.get("device", "NPU")), priority=int(npu.get("priority", 1)),
CHANGE TO: gpu = config_data.get("gpu", {}) ... model_dir = self._resolve_path(repo_root, service_root, str(gpu.get("model_dir", ""))) manifest_raw = str(gpu.get("weight_manifest", "")).strip() ... device=str(gpu.get("device", "GPU")), priority=int(gpu.get("priority", 1)),
C) UPDATE _validate_config_data() (lines ~423-440): CURRENT: npu = self._require_section_dict(config_data, "npu", code="AO_CFG_NPU_SECTION_MISSING") device = self._require_non_empty_str(npu, "device", code="AO_CFG_NPU_DEVICE_MISSING") if device.upper() != "NPU": raise ConfigResolutionError( code="AO_CFG_DEVICE_INVALID", message=f"Orchestrator device must be NPU per ADR-010, got '{device}'.", ) self._require_non_empty_str(npu, "model_dir", code="AO_CFG_MODEL_DIR_MISSING") self._require_int_range(npu, "priority", minimum=1, maximum=7, code="AO_CFG_PRIORITY_INVALID")
CHANGE TO: gpu = self._require_section_dict(config_data, "gpu", code="AO_CFG_GPU_SECTION_MISSING") device = self._require_non_empty_str(gpu, "device", code="AO_CFG_GPU_DEVICE_MISSING") if device.upper() != "GPU": raise ConfigResolutionError( code="AO_CFG_DEVICE_INVALID", message=f"Orchestrator device must be GPU per ADR-011, got '{device}'.", ) self._require_non_empty_str(gpu, "model_dir", code="AO_CFG_MODEL_DIR_MISSING") self._require_int_range(gpu, "priority", minimum=1, maximum=7, code="AO_CFG_PRIORITY_INVALID")
D) UPDATE the dev_mode=false weight_manifest check (line ~497): CURRENT: weight_manifest = npu.get("weight_manifest") CHANGE TO: weight_manifest = gpu.get("weight_manifest")
E) UPDATE all OrchestratorNPUInference references in start() method: CURRENT (line ~226): inference = OrchestratorNPUInference( model_dir=str(resolved.model_dir), ... ) CHANGE TO: inference = OrchestratorGPUInference( model_dir=str(resolved.model_dir), ... )
F) UPDATE module-level docstring: Replace references to "NPU model" → "GPU model" and "OrchestratorNPUInference" → "OrchestratorGPUInference".
G) UPDATE __init__.py: In services/assistant_orchestrator/src/__init__.py, update the docstring to reflect GPU (replace "NPU Sole Consumer (ADR-010)" with "GPU Inference (ADR-011)").

item.title: Update TOML configs to Qwen3-14B model paths
item.description:
A) services/assistant_orchestrator/config/default.toml: CURRENT [gpu] section: model_dir = "models/qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct/openvino-int4-npu" # PROVISIONAL weight_manifest = "models/qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct/openvino-int4-npu/manifest.json" # PROVISIONAL max_context_tokens = 4096
CHANGE TO: model_dir = "models/qwen3-14b/openvino-int4-gpu" weight_manifest = "models/qwen3-14b/openvino-int4-gpu/manifest.json" max_context_tokens = 16384 priority = 1
Remove the "# PROVISIONAL" comments. Update max_context_tokens from 4096 to 16384 per DEC-03. Add priority = 1 to the [gpu] section (required by _validate_config_data() after WI-8 changes the validator to read the [gpu] section).
B) services/assistant_orchestrator/config/guest_runtime.toml: Same model_dir and weight_manifest updates. Same max_context_tokens → 16384 update. Add priority = 1 to the [gpu] section. Remove PROVISIONAL comments.

context_notes.note: The PA's gpu_inference.py (services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py) was updated in M5.1 and serves as the REFERENCE PATTERN for the AO's load_model() rewrite. Key differences between PA and AO: - PA uses priority_hint = "HIGH" if priority == 0 else "LOW" - AO uses priority_hint = "MEDIUM" if priority <= 1 else "LOW" (retain AO's existing priority logic — do not copy PA's) - PA does NOT have streaming, preemption detection, or KV-cache. AO has all three. Do NOT remove any AO-specific features. - PA classify_car() sets num_assistant_tokens directly in gen_config. AO sets it in _build_generation_config() — the centralized config builder method. This is the correct location for AO.

The OrchestratorNPUInference = OrchestratorGPUInference alias at the end of gpu_inference.py ensures that: - test_npu_inference.py (imports OrchestratorNPUInference by name) continues to work - test_entrypoint.py @patch decorators referencing OrchestratorNPUInference continue to work UNTIL M5.3 updates those tests. For NOW, the test_entrypoint.py @patch targets will need to reference the NEW import path. See CN-5. - External scripts (phase2_gates/scripts/*.py) continue to work

The TOML configs already use [gpu] section headers. The Python code still reads config_data.get("npu", {}). When the TOML parser returns the config dict, there is no "npu" key — only "gpu". So: - _load_entrypoint_config() gets an empty dict for npu → empty model_dir - _validate_config_data() calls _require_section_dict("npu") → raises ConfigResolutionError("AO_CFG_NPU_SECTION_MISSING") - AO fails to start → gateway handshake times out → GATEWAY_HANDSHAKE_FAILED WI-8 fixes this by reading "gpu" instead of "npu".

After M5.2, the AO test suite should still pass (150 tests) because: - test_npu_inference.py imports OrchestratorNPUInference by name from the OLD module path (services.assistant_orchestrator.src.npu_inference). After git mv, this import path breaks. HOWEVER: Python's import system resolves the file on disk. Since the file was renamed, the import `from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.npu_inference import ...` will FAIL.
SOLUTION: The backward-compat alias is inside gpu_inference.py. But the IMPORT PATH still says npu_inference.py — which no longer exists on disk. Therefore, you MUST also create a thin shim file at the OLD path:
services/assistant_orchestrator/src/npu_inference.py
containing ONLY: """Backward-compatibility shim — redirects to gpu_inference.py (M5.3 removes).""" from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.gpu_inference import * # noqa: F401,F403 from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.gpu_inference import ( OrchestratorGPUInference as OrchestratorNPUInference, # noqa: F401 )
This ensures test_npu_inference.py, test_entrypoint.py, and all external scripts continue to resolve imports until M5.3 cleans them up.

note.title: test_entrypoint.py patch targets after file rename

context_notes.note:
test_entrypoint.py has 3 @patch decorators: @patch("services.assistant_orchestrator.src.entrypoint.OrchestratorNPUInference")
After WI-8 changes the import in entrypoint.py to: from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.gpu_inference import OrchestratorGPUInference
The entrypoint module's namespace will have OrchestratorGPUInference (not NPU). The existing @patch targets will no longer match.
SOLUTION: The test file needs these patches updated to: @patch("services.assistant_orchestrator.src.entrypoint.OrchestratorGPUInference")
HOWEVER: test_entrypoint.py is an M5.3 target. To avoid scope creep, there are two options: Option A (PREFERRED): Update the 3 @patch targets in test_entrypoint.py as part of M5.2 (minimal, mechanical change — 3 lines). Option B: Add OrchestratorNPUInference as an alias in the entrypoint.py import block temporarily.
Use Option A — update the 3 @patch decorators in test_entrypoint.py now. This is a 3-line mechanical change and avoids polluting entrypoint.py with temporary aliases.
Similarly, update the _write_minimal_config() helper in test_entrypoint.py: its TOML template currently uses [npu] with device = "NPU". After WI-8, the config resolver expects [gpu] with device = "GPU". Update: [npu] → [gpu] device = "NPU" → device = "GPU" in ALL _write_minimal_config() and inline TOML string fixtures in test_entrypoint.py.

npu_inference.py contains module-level functions _softmax() and _sample_token() that are imported by test_npu_inference.py. These are UNCHANGED by M5.2 — preserve them exactly. They must be importable from both gpu_inference.py and the npu_inference.py shim.
Update the unload() log from "Orchestrator NPU model unloaded" to "Orchestrator GPU model unloaded".

gate.pass_criteria: 150 passed (AO baseline), 0 failed. All AO tests must pass — the backward-compat shim + test_entrypoint.py patch updates ensure this.
≥749 passed, ≤2 failed (pre-existing: test_build_prompt_does_not_contain_no_think, test_stop_token_ids_constants_defined). Any new failures = STOP.

commit_template.message:
feat(ao): M5.2 — AO GPU rewrite + speculative decoding + config resolver fix
- Rename npu_inference.py → gpu_inference.py (git mv + shim) - Rename OrchestratorNPUInference → OrchestratorGPUInference - Rewrite load_model(): GPU + draft_model + SchedulerConfig + compile props - Update GenerationConfig defaults: deterministic (do_sample=False, temp=0.0) - Wire num_assistant_tokens=3 in _build_generation_config() (DEC-01) - Overhaul entrypoint.py config resolver: npu→gpu section reads (fixes GATEWAY_HANDSHAKE_FAILED) - Update TOML configs: Qwen3-14B model paths, max_context_tokens=16384 - Update AO constants.py with model re-exports - Update test_entrypoint.py: patch targets + TOML fixtures (npu→gpu)
ADR-011, ADR-012, DEC-01, DEC-03, DEC-05, DEC-06, DEC-07 Test: 150 AO passed | Regression: ≥749 passed, ≤2 pre-existing failures
commit_template.instruction: git add -A git commit -m "feat(ao): M5.2 — AO GPU rewrite + speculative decoding + config resolver fix ..."

verification.step: python -c "import tomllib; d=tomllib.load(open('services/assistant_orchestrator/config/default.toml','rb')); assert 'gpu' in d; assert d['gpu']['device']=='GPU'; assert 'qwen3-14b' in d['gpu']['model_dir']; assert d['gpu']['priority']==1; print('CONFIG OK')"
python -c "from services.assistant_orchestrator.src.gpu_inference import OrchestratorGPUInference; print('GPU IMPORT OK')"
